Cards.Back: SOLID is an acronym for five design principles: Single Responsibility, Open/Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, and Dependency Inversion.
Cards.ExampleSentence: Use SOLID principles to write clean code.

Cards.Back: Merge keeps full commit history with merge commits. Rebase rewrites commit history on top of the target branch for a linear project history.
Cards.Mnemonic: Rebase = rewrite history, Merge = preserve history
Cards.ExampleSentence: Rebasing keeps the commit tree clean.
